steps.title: No solution means parallel lines: same slope, different intercept
steps.content:
Rewrite the given equation in slope-intercept form. Starting with \(2x = 20y - 8\), divide both sides by 2:
$$x = 10y - 4 \implies y = \frac{1}{10}x + \frac{2}{5}$$
Slope = \(\frac{1}{10}\), y-intercept = \(\frac{2}{5}\). The second equation must have slope \(\frac{1}{10}\) and a different y-intercept.

steps.title: Test all four choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — \(x = 5y\): Rewrite: \(y = rac{1}{5}x\). Slope = \( rac{1}{5} eq rac{1}{10}\). Lines intersect (one solution). ✗
  • Choice B — \( rac{1}{2}x = 5y\): Rewrite: \(y = rac{1}{10}x\). Slope = \( rac{1}{10}\) ✓, y-intercept = 0 \( eq rac{2}{5}\) ✓. Parallel lines → no solution. ✓
  • Choice C — \(x = 10y - 4\): Rewrite: \(y = rac{1}{10}x + rac{2}{5}\). Slope = \( rac{1}{10}\) and y-intercept = \( rac{2}{5}\) — identical to the given equation. This gives infinitely many solutions, not no solution. ✗
  • Choice D — \( rac{1}{2}x = 10y - 4\): Rewrite: \(x = 20y - 8 \implies y = rac{1}{20}x + rac{2}{5}\). Slope = \( rac{1}{20} eq rac{1}{10}\). Lines intersect. ✗

steps.title: Conclusion
steps.content: Only Choice B produces a line with slope \(\frac{1}{10}\) and a different y-intercept (0 vs \(\frac{2}{5}\)), making the lines parallel with no solution. That matches choice B — B is correct.
